prose: Fukui reactivity indices for phenol computed at the DFT B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard') using PySCF RKS. Gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Density fitting was OFF (exact four-center integrals). Partial charges via Mulliken (PySCF). N−1 cation state: charge +1, multiplicity 2; N+1 anion state: charge −1, multiplicity 2. Geometry: input .xyz at fixed coordinates (no re-optimization). All integrity checks passed (trustworthy: true). Charge conservation: Σf⁺ = 1.0000, Σf⁻ = 1.0000. No engine warnings. Most electrophilic site: atom 3 (C), f⁺ = 2.1028319. Most nucleophilic site: atom 1 (O), f⁻ = 0.16201477. The headline value reported under 'value' is the maximum f⁺ (most electrophilic carbon, atom 3). The ortho/para carbons (atoms 3, 4, 7) show the highest electrophilicity, consistent with the hydroxyl group's ortho/para-directing effect in SEAr chemistry.
